key_themes: Capital recycling, asset sales, and €100m share buyback program
Strategic pivot to green digital infrastructure / data centers for AI energy demand
Operational challenges from low wind speeds and power prices in 2025
Dividend stability despite swinging to a loss
Addressing NAV discount and rebuilding shareholder value
summary: Greencoat Renewables (GRP.IR / GRP.L), a European renewable energy infrastructure fund, reported a challenging FY2025 with lower generation, reduced power prices, a pretax loss, and NAV decline, but management responded aggressively with plans to sell assets, return €100m to shareholders via buybacks, reduce debt, and launch a green digital infrastructure platform targeting data centers and AI power needs. The market reacted positively with a share price jump on the announcements, director buying, and maintained dividend, shifting sentiment toward optimism around value unlocking despite near-term headwinds. Social and investor chatter is limited but constructive, focusing on the bold capital allocation strategy.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/greencoat-renewables-plc-lse-grp-170037651.html)[[2]](https://quoteddata.com/2026/03/greencoat-renewables-to-return-e100m-to-shareholders/)[[3]](https://www.morningstar.com/news/alliance-news/1772706842019234200/greencoat-renewables-launches-buyback-after-swinging-to-annual-loss)
sources: 10+ recent news articles (Yahoo Finance, GuruFocus, QuotedData, reNews, company announcements on FY2025 results, buybacks, and new platform), 5 relevant X posts discussing buybacks, director purchases, and strategic plan, Investor discussion boards (ADVFN, Stockopedia, analyst notes)
